title: Box Office Future: AMC Encouraged by Studios' Plans To Increase Movies
text:
Studio Commitment to Expanded Film Slates Provides Confidence for Theater Recovery

AMC Entertainment expressed strong encouragement regarding studios' expansion of theatrical releases planned for 2025 and 2026. This represents a critical development for the theater industry's recovery prospects.

Studio Expansion Plans: Multiple studios including Universal have committed to expanding theatrical release schedules. Universal specifically announced plans to extend theatrical windows—the period before streaming release—from 17 days to 30 days in 2026 and 45 days by 2027. This represents a major victory for exhibitors after pandemic-era compression of theatrical windows.

Window Extension Impact: Theater owners view the extended windows as crucial for rebuilding audience habits after the pandemic had compressed release schedules to minimal theatrical runs. The 45-day window represents a return toward pre-pandemic norms, providing theaters sufficient time to capitalize on major releases.

Expanded Release Slate: Cinema Distribution partners report expectations for increased wide releases across the industry. Studios are committing to more theatrical releases per year, providing theaters with a deeper content pipeline for driving attendance and concession sales.

Industry Cooperation: The improved cooperation between studios and exhibitors reflects shared interest in rebuilding the theatrical exhibition business. Theater chains like AMC view the planned film slate expansions as validating their recovery investments and debt restructuring decisions.
